question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: loadAll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the systemFiles map is keyed by genesisFile().fileId() (constructed from EntityId shard/realm/num), so that if two system entities share the same encoded fileId (due to shard/realm encoding collision), one overwrites the other in the Collectors.toMap() call, causing the wrong genesis file to be returned for a system file lookup and direct loss of funds through incorrect fee computation?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that genesisNetworkProperties is always returned from memory (bypassing DB) for the networkPropertyFile, so that if the genesis network properties contain a lower maxGasPerSec or different fee multiplier than the current live network, the user can submit transactions that are priced using stale network parameters and underpay fees, resulting in direct loss of funds to the network?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: loadWithRetry(FileID, long, SystemFile)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a denial-of-service on the fee schedule loading path by submitting many concurrent requests that all trigger ParseException on the feeScheduleFile, causing 10 DB queries per request (maxRetries=9) and exhausting DB connection pool, so that legitimate users cannot get correct fee schedules and their transactions fail or are priced incorrectly, leading to direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the CacheKey.toString() method uses EntityId.of(fileId.shardNum(), fileId.realmNum(), fileId.fileNum()) for logging, and if the fileId contains unexpected values, the toString() could throw an exception that propagates and causes the load() to fail, falling back to genesis fee schedule and resulting in direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that for the exchangeRateFile, the consensusTimestamp is rounded down to the hour before being used as a DB query parameter in getFileAtTimestamp(), so that the DB query retrieves the exchange rate that was valid at the start of the hour rather than the one valid at the actual transaction time, causing the user to be charged based on a potentially much older exchange rate and suffering direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: loadWithRetry(FileID, long, SystemFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the codec validation (codec.parse()) only checks protobuf structural validity but not semantic validity of the ExchangeRateSet (e.g., does not verify that hbarEquiv > 0 or centEquiv > 0), so that a DB entry with a zero-denominator exchange rate passes codec.parse() and is cached, causing division-by-zero or zero-cost gas computation and direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the cache.put(cacheKey, result) call stores the File object without any expiry override, relying entirely on the CacheManager's configured TTL, so that if the TTL is set to a very large value (or infinity), a stale exchange rate or fee schedule is served indefinitely and all users' transactions are priced incorrectly, resulting in direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: loadAll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the genesis exchange rate (fileSchema.genesisExchangeRatesBytes(configuration)) is hardcoded at application startup and never refreshed, so that if the application runs for a long time and the real exchange rate diverges significantly from genesis, any fallback to genesisFile() for the exchangeRateFile causes the user's gas to be priced at the genesis rate and direct loss of funds occurs?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that isSystemFile() is checked in FileReadableKVState using the same systemFiles map, but load() is called with currentTimestamp (not the historical timestamp from ContractCallContext), so that a historical contract call simulation always uses the current-time system files instead of the historically correct ones, causing incorrect fee computation for historical simulations and direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: loadWithRetry(FileID, long, SystemFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that when all 10 retry attempts fail with InvalidFileException, the catch(RetryException e) block silently returns systemFile.genesisFile() without any error propagation to the caller, so that the caller (e.g., MidnightRatesSingleton.get()) receives the genesis ExchangeRateSet and uses it for fee computation, causing the user to be charged at genesis-era rates and direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the CacheKey record's equals/hashCode is based on (FileID, long timestamp), and FileID equality depends on shardNum/realmNum/fileNum, so that a crafted request with a FileID that has the same fileNum but different shard/realm as the real exchangeRateFileId bypasses the exchangeRatesCacheManager branch and uses the defaultSystemFileCacheManager instead, causing the exchange rate to be loaded without hour-rounding and potentially returning a different (wrong) cached value, leading to direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: maxExpiry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that maxExpiry() uses Instant.now().getEpochSecond() + maxLifetime to set the expirationSecond on genesis files, so that genesis files always appear valid and non-expired regardless of the actual network state, causing the EVM to accept and use genesis fee schedules that should have expired, resulting in direct loss of funds through incorrect fee computation?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the load() method does not validate that the returned File's contents are non-null or non-empty before caching and returning them, so that a File with empty contents is cached and returned to the EVM, causing the fee schedule or exchange rate to be parsed as empty/default and all transactions to be priced at zero or default rates, resulting in direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: loadWithRetry(FileID, long, SystemFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the retry predicate only catches InvalidFileException (wrapping ParseException), but not other runtime exceptions (e.g., NullPointerException, DataAccessException from the DB), so that a DB error causes an uncaught exception to propagate out of loadWithRetry() and the caller falls back to an error state that uses genesis fee data, resulting in direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the exchangeRatesCacheManager and defaultSystemFileCacheManager are both Caffeine-based and share the same CACHE_NAME ('default'), so that if the Spring cache abstraction routes a request to the wrong CacheManager, a fee schedule entry is retrieved from the exchange rate cache or vice versa, causing the EVM to use the wrong file for fee computation and direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: loadAll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the systemFiles map is populated once at lazy initialization and never refreshed, so that if the system entity IDs (exchangeRateFile, feeScheduleFile, etc.) change at runtime (e.g., due to a network upgrade), the stale FileID keys in the map cause lookups to miss and always return genesis files, resulting in direct loss of funds through incorrect fee computation?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the load() method is public and takes a raw long consensusTimestamp without any bounds checking, so that passing a negative timestamp causes roundDownToHour() to produce a negative cache key, which may collide with a valid cache entry or cause unexpected DB query behavior, leading to wrong fee data being returned and direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: loadWithRetry(FileID, long, SystemFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the File returned by loadWithRetry() is built with File.newBuilder().contents(bytes).fileId(key).build() without setting expirationSecond, so that the returned File has a null/zero expiration and the EVM treats it as expired, causing the fee schedule to be rejected and a fallback to genesis rates, resulting in direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the cache is populated with the result of loadFromDB() only when result != null, but if loadFromDB() returns null (systemFile not found in map), the null is not cached and every subsequent request re-queries the DB, creating a cache-bypass DoS that exhausts DB connections and causes legitimate fee schedule lookups to fail, resulting in transactions being priced at genesis rates and direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the MidnightRatesSingleton.get() calls systemFileLoader.load(exchangeRateFileId, timestamp) and then immediately calls ExchangeRateSet.PROTOBUF.parse(file.contents()) without null-checking the returned file, so that if load() returns null (cache miss + DB miss), a NullPointerException propagates and the fee computation falls back to a default zero-rate, causing the user to pay zero gas fees and direct loss of funds to the network?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: loadWithRetry(FileID, long, SystemFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the nanoSeconds AtomicLong is decremented by 1 nanosecond per retry (nanoSeconds.set(fileData.getConsensusTimestamp() - 1)), so that if the DB has a corrupt entry at timestamp T and a valid entry at T-1, the retry succeeds with the T-1 entry, but if T-1 is a very old exchange rate (e.g., from years ago), the user's gas is priced at the historical rate and direct loss of funds occurs?
